ATTY. JOHN M. HARDING OF BURGESS, MILLS & RING, TEL. NO. 982-6393 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE UNIVERISTY.

RULING - Plaintiff LEE ROBINS'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Inspection Demand, Set Two; And In The Alternative For Issue Sanctions IS DENIED AS IT SEEKS PORTFOLIOS, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE PORTFOLIO OF THE CANDIDATE WHO HAS AGREED TO THE PRODUCTION OF THAT PERSON'S PORTFOLIO. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-12-2007 Continued To Aug-11-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Inspection Demand, Set One, Nos. 3,6,7.8,10,11; Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One, Nos. 215.1,215.2; And Special Interrogatories, Set One, No. 15
Off Calendar Feb-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Lee Robbins' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Inspection Demand, Set One; Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One; And Special Interrogatories, Set One, is granted. Defendant is ordered to make production of documents that exist and have been located in compliance with C.C.P. Section 2031.210 and 2031.230 and to verify that all responsive documents have been produced within 15 days of notice of this order. The production must be made of all documents located at the time of the production and verification.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatories Numbers 215.1 and 215.2 and Defendant's objections are overruled. While the content of interviews may be privileged, the existence of interviews is not privileged.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Number 15 as the objections are ordered stricken. The response otherwise may be adequate. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-12-2007 Continued To Jul-23-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 504

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quahs Subpoena For Production Of Personal Records; Rule 3.1020 Statement In Support Of His Motion
Off Calendar Nov-14-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Dean Royer, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Lee Robbins
John M. Harding, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Golden Gate University

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Lee Robbin's Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Personal Records is granted in part and denied in part. The motion to quash is denied. The subpoena is limited to treatment arising out defendant's adverse employment actions. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-16-2007 Set For Trial On Feb-19-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jan-30-08 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 504
